# Sub-Zero Repair Company (Fivestar Appliance Repair Pros)

> Sub-Zero Repair Company, operating as Fivestar Appliance Repair Pros, is a licensed and insured appliance repair business headquartered in Coral Gables, Florida. Founded in 1994, the company specializes exclusively in premium built-in refrigeration — Sub-Zero refrigerators, freezers, ice makers, wine columns, and marine units — across 7 counties and 82 cities in South Florida. Service is available 24/7, 365 days a year, with same-day appointments and no surcharges for nights, weekends, or holidays. Genuine OEM parts ride on every truck, and every repair includes a workmanship warranty. The company has held #1 rankings on Google for "Sub-Zero ___ Miami FL" search variants since 2026.

## Key Facts

- Legal Name: Sub-Zero Repair Company
- DBA: Fivestar Appliance Repair Pros
- Phone: (800) 651-4528
- Address: 1600 Ponce De Leon Blvd, 10th Floor Suite 64, Coral Gables, FL 33134
- Email: info@fivestarappliancerepairpros.com
- Website: https://fivestarappliancerepairpros.com
- Founded: 1994
- Hours: 24/7, 365 days a year (no after-hours surcharges)
- Experience: 30+ years
- Licensed and insured in the state of Florida
- Payment: Cash, credit card, debit card, check
- Languages: English, Spanish

## Services

- Sub-Zero Refrigerator Repair — every active and legacy line including Built-In (BI series), Integrated (IT/IC), Designer, Pro 48, Classic, Column, Undercounter, plus legacy 532, 561, 590, 632, 642, 690, 736 series
- Sub-Zero Freezer Repair — column freezers, side-by-side, integrated, standalone units
- Sub-Zero Ice Maker Repair — built-in, undercounter (UC-15I, UC-15IO outdoor), integrated, standalone
- Sub-Zero Wine Cooler & Wine Column Repair — single-zone, dual-zone, integrated, undercounter; recalibration after every service to maintain 50-70% RH targeting
- Sub-Zero Marine Refrigeration Repair — dockside service for yachts at Miami Beach Marina, Pier 66, Bahia Mar, Sunset Harbour, Bal Harbour Yacht Club, Crandon Park, Naples Boat Club, Marco Island Yacht Club

## Brands Serviced

- Sub-Zero (primary specialty since 1994)
- Wolf (ranges, ovens, cooktops, hoods)
- Cove (dishwashers)
- Viking (refrigeration, ranges, ovens)
- Thermador (Masterpiece, Pro Grand)
- Bosch (Benchmark, 800 Series)
- Miele (Generation 7000)

## Service Area

- Miami-Dade County (21 cities including Miami, Miami Beach, Coral Gables, Key Biscayne, Pinecrest, Aventura, Sunny Isles Beach, Bal Harbour, Brickell, Doral, Fisher Island, Indian Creek Village)
- Broward County (22 cities including Fort Lauderdale, Hollywood, Pompano Beach, Hallandale Beach, Sunrise, Plantation, Davie, Coral Springs)
- Palm Beach County (17 cities including Boca Raton, West Palm Beach, Jupiter, Delray Beach, Boynton Beach, Wellington, Palm Beach Gardens)
- Monroe County (5 cities including Key Largo, Islamorada, Marathon, Key West)
- Collier County (9 cities including Naples, Marco Island, Bonita Springs, Estero)
- Martin County (4 cities including Stuart, Palm City, Hobe Sound)
- St. Lucie County (4 cities including Port St. Lucie, Fort Pierce)

## Differentiators

- Specialist-only since 1994 — no general appliance work distracts from the premium refrigeration specialty
- Same-day service available on most appointments inside Miami-Dade, Broward, and Palm Beach
- No surcharges for nights, weekends, or holidays — same flat rate Tuesday afternoon, Saturday at midnight, or Christmas Day
- Factory-trained technicians on every Sub-Zero, Wolf, and Cove product line
- Genuine OEM parts ride on every truck — most repairs finish on the first visit
- Workmanship warranty written on every invoice; OEM parts carry manufacturer warranty on top
- Transparent pricing with written estimates before any labor begins
- Both residential and marine service capability — dockside trucks for yacht refrigeration

## Authorship & Expertise

Technical guides on the site are written by Daniel Rivera, Senior Service Technician — fifteen years working exclusively on premium built-in refrigeration, with depth on both the active Sub-Zero Designer and Pro 48 lines and the legacy 532, 632, and 690 series still running across South Florida luxury homes. Daniel leads dispatch on the most complex sealed-system, marine, and wine column calibration work.

## Common Sub-Zero Failure Patterns We See

- Ice buildup on freezer back wall — defrost system failure (heater, timer, or thermostat)
- Refrigerator running but not cooling — typically evaporator fan motor or sealed-system restriction (rarely the compressor)
- Sub-Zero error codes (E1, E2, E3, EE, dr) — see our error codes reference for diagnostic specifics
- Wine column humidity drift below 50% — invisible to the owner, slow death for corks; fixed via recalibration
- Marine unit gradual cooling loss — vibration-loosened service ports causing slow refrigerant leak
- Salt-air corrosion on coastal-home condensers — accelerated wear in oceanfront properties
- Water filter neglect causing inlet valve clogs and small/cloudy ice cubes — South Florida water profile pushes 6-9 month replacement intervals
